codeReview.summary: The PYQ library feature implements the basic structure but has several blocking issues. Backend pagination counts are incorrect when filtering by year. The practice/exam modes incorrectly handle the URL id parameter (it fetches a list but uses id to find one). Frontend uses hardcoded mock data for subjects/topics instead of API. Bookmarks are not persisted. The feature does not follow TDD approach (no tests added despite skill requirement).

issues.description: When filtering PYQs by year, get_question_count() is called without year filter, returning incorrect total count for pagination. This causes wrong pagination metadata - total will be all questions for subject/difficulty, not PYQs for that specific year.
Search endpoint uses 'total = len(questions)' which only counts returned page items, not total matching results. This breaks pagination for search results.
get_available_years() calls get_question_count() without year filter for each year, so all years show identical counts. The year_counts should be per-year, not global.

The practice page fetches a list of PYQs but uses params.id to find one specific PYQ. The logic 'const currentPYQ = pyqs.find(q => q.id === pyqId) || pyqs[0]' means if id doesn't match, it falls back to first item. The URL id parameter is effectively ignored - all filtered PYQs load and user navigates through them, making the id meaningless.
Same issue as practice page - fetches list but uses params.id to find one. The id parameter is ignored since the component shows a list and navigates through all.
Uses hardcoded MOCK_SUBJECTS array with static IDs instead of fetching subjects from API. Topic filter also has hardcoded options. This means subject/topic filtering doesn't work with real data.
Bookmarks are stored only in local React state (Set<string>) and never persisted to backend. User loses bookmarked PYQs on page reload.
Unused variable 'topic_ids' is assigned empty list then never used. Dead code.
Import of 'status' from fastapi is unused.
No test files were added for the PYQ feature. The frontend-worker skill requires TDD with failing tests first, but no tests were created in apps/web/src/__tests__/

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker did not follow TDD procedure from frontend-worker skill. Skill explicitly requires 'Write failing tests first (red phase)' but worker added no tests. skillFeedback.followedProcedure is true, but this appears incorrect since no test files exist.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Skill says 'Create component test file in apps/web/src/__tests__/' and 'Write failing tests for expected rendering/interactions'. Handoff shows 'tests': {'added': [], 'coverage': 'No new test files added'}.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The skill requires agent-browser verification, but handoff shows only curl verification was performed. interactiveChecks array is empty despite skill requiring browser validation of page rendering and interactions.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff verification shows commandsRun with curl to localhost:3101/pyqs, but interactiveChecks is empty [] while skill requires agent-browser verification for desktop and mobile viewports.

sharedStateObservations.observation: AGENTS.md specifies 'Clean Code: No giant files. Max 200-300 lines per file.' but exam/page.tsx is 620 lines and practice/page.tsx is 468 lines.

summary: The PYQ library feature has significant implementation gaps. Backend has pagination bugs where year-filtered counts are wrong. Frontend practice/exam modes misuse URL id parameter. Mock data used instead of real API for subjects/topics. Bookmarks aren't persisted. No tests despite TDD requirement in skill. The feature compiles and basic structure exists, but core functionality like correct filtering, pagination accuracy, and bookmark persistence is broken or missing.
